criteria.name: F1
criteria.description: Data is assigned a globally unique persistent identifier

metric.name: M37
metric.description: Data is assigned a globally unique persistent identifier

tests.name: T1
tests.description: Data MUST be assigned a globally unique identifier
tests.text: Is your data(set) assigned a globally unique persistent and resolvable identifier?

criteria.name: F2
criteria.description: Metadata includes descriptive core elements (creator, title, data identifier, publisher, publication date, summary and keywords) to support data findability.

metric.name: M38
metric.description: Metadata includes descriptive core elements (creator, title, data identifier, publisher, publication date, summary and keywords) to support data findability.

tests.name: T2
tests.description: Metadata MUST include descriptive core elements (creator, title, data identifier, publisher, publication date, summary and keywords) to support data findability.
tests.text: Did you provide discovery metadata in order to make the data(set) findable, understandable and reusable to others?

criteria.name: F3
criteria.description: Metadata includes the identifier of the data it describes.

metric.name: M51
metric.description: Metadata includes the identifier of the data it describes.

tests.name: T12
tests.description: The metadata MUST include the identifier of the data or object it describes
tests.text: Does the metadata also include the identifier of the dataset it describes?

principles.name: Findable
principles.description: The first step in (re)using data is to find them. Metadata and data should be easy to find for both humans and computers. Machine-readable metadata are essential for automatic discovery of datasets and services, so this is an essential component of the FAIRification process.

criteria.name: A1
criteria.description: Metadata contains access level and access conditions of the data.

metric.name: M47
metric.description: Metadata contains access level and access conditions of the data.

tests.name: T13
tests.description: The metadata SHOULD include access conditions, if applicable, and preferably include these access conditions in the licence.
tests.text: Does the metadata or the licence describe access conditions, if applicable?

tests.name: T14
tests.description: This is a dummy test to show multiple tests per criterion.
tests.text: Is this a dummy test to show multiple tests per criterion?

criteria.name: A2
criteria.description: Metadata remains available, even if the data is no longer available.

metric.name: M41
metric.description: Metadata remains available, even if the data is no longer available.

tests.name: T5
tests.description: Metadata MUST remain accessible even if data is no longer available.
tests.text: Do you ensure that the metadata remains available over time, even if the data(set) is no longer accessible?

principles.name: Accessible
principles.description: Once the user finds the required data, she/he/they need to know how they can be accessed, possibly including authentication and authorisation.

criteria.name: I1
criteria.description: Metadata is represented using a formal knowledge representation language.

metric.name: M42
metric.description: Metadata is represented using a formal knowledge representation language.

tests.name: T7
tests.description: Metadata SHOULD be represented using a formal knowledge representation language.
tests.text: Does the metadata describing your data(set) conform to or is it transformable to a formal knowledge representation language?

tests.name: T14
tests.description: Metadata SHOULD maximally make use of appropriate vocabularies.
tests.text: Does the metadata describing your data(set) use controlled vocabularies whenever appolicable?

principles.name: Interoperable
principles.description: The data usually need to be integrated with other data. In addition, the data need to interoperate with applications or workflows for analysis, storage, and processing.

criteria.name: R1
criteria.description: (Meta)data are richly described with a plurality of accurate and relevant attributes

metric.name: M50
metric.description: (Meta)data are richly described with a plurality of accurate and relevant attributes

tests.name: T16
tests.description: Metadata SHOULD support reuse with rich metadata, describing detailed aspects of methodology, variables, instruments, and data schema.
tests.text: Does the metadata support reusability with descriptions of methids, instruments, and a data dictionary?

criteria.name: R2
criteria.description: Metadata includes licence information under which data can be reused.

metric.name: M40
metric.description: Metadata includes licence information under which data can be reused.

tests.name: T4
tests.description: Metadata MUST include licence information under which data can be reused.
tests.text: Does the metadata include the licence information under which the data(set) can be reused?

criteria.name: R1.1
criteria.description: Metadata includes provenance information about data creation or generation.

metric.name: M44
metric.description: Metadata includes provenance information about data creation or generation.

tests.name: T8
tests.text: Do you include or reference provenance information about the collection and/or generation of data in the metadata?

criteria.name: R1.2
criteria.description: Metadata follows a standard recommended by the target research community of the data.

metric.name: M45
metric.description: Metadata follows a standard recommended by the target research community of the data.

tests.name: T9
tests.description: Metadata SHOULD include provenance information about data creation or generation.
tests.text: Is the metadata describing your data(set) based on specifications or a standard that is community-endorsed ?

criteria.name: R1.3
criteria.description: Data is available in a file format recommended by the target research community.

metric.name: M46
metric.description: Data is available in a file format recommended by the target research community.

tests.name: T10
tests.description: File formats SHOULD follow a standard recommended by the target research community.
tests.text: Is your data(set) deposited in a file format that is open and supported by the data repository for long-term preservation?

metric.name: M49
metric.description: Data curation is the active and ongoing management of data to ensure that it’s available for discovery and reuse. This process covers the entire lifecycle of the data(set), starting at the selection or creation and continuing on for as long as the data(set) exists.

tests.name: T11
tests.description: Data SHOULD be offered in one or more file formats recommended by the target community.
tests.text: Does the repository you have deposited in provide professional data curation and digital preservation?

principles.name: Reusable
principles.description: The ultimate goal of FAIR is to optimise the reuse of data. To achieve this, metadata and data should be well-described so that they can be replicated and/or combined in different settings.
